column1: Hello, my daughter has been complaining of a headache since she came back from kindergarten this afternoon. I just took her temperature and it's 38.6°C (101.5°F). I found a bottle of liquid Acetaminophen (Tylenol) in our medicine cabinet, but the box is gone and I don't know the exact dosage. She is 4 and a half years old and weighs about 35 lbs (16 kg). Could you tell me approximately how many milliliters I should pour for her? Is there anything else I can do besides giving her medicine?

Hello doctor, my grandmother is 72 years old and has had diabetes for over ten years. After dinner today, she suddenly said her stomach felt very uncomfortable, like a heavy stone was pressing right under her chest. She keeps breaking out in a cold sweat and feels nauseous but can't throw up. We have Omeprazole and some emergency heart pills at home. Is her stomach ulcer acting up again? Can I give her two Omeprazole pills first?
